stem: A 50-foot ladder is leaned against a building so that the angle between the ladder and the ground is 37 degrees. Using the approximations sin 37° ≈ 3/5 and cos 37° ≈ 4/5, what is the height, in feet, that the top of the ladder reaches on the building?
explanation: In this right triangle, the ladder is the hypotenuse (50 feet) and the height on the building is the side opposite the 37° angle. Using sin 37° = opposite/hypotenuse: opposite = 50 × (3/5) = 30 feet. This is a 3-4-5 Pythagorean triple scaled by 10.

distractorRationale.A: Halved the hypotenuse instead of multiplying by sin, computing 50 ÷ 2 = 25.
distractorRationale.B: Used cos 37° ≈ 4/5 instead of sin 37° to find the opposite side, computing 50 × (4/5) = 40.
distractorRationale.D: Subtracted the base distance from the hypotenuse instead of multiplying by sin, computing 50 - 40 = 10.
